meaning: To set a specific future time for an email, message, or calendar event to be automatically sent or held (strong alternative to 'send later' or 'email later').
example: I will schedule the client onboarding email to go out on Monday at 9:00 AM.

meaning: To send a brief, direct message to someone, usually on a chat platform, to get their quick attention (strong alternative to 'send a message' or 'get in touch with').
example: Please ping me on Slack once the server is back up.

meaning: To coordinate two or more devices, files, or teams so they contain the exact same updated information (strong alternative to 'make the same' or 'get everyone updated').
example: We need to synchronize our calendars before booking the client presentations.

meaning: To join a brief, informal online meeting to resolve an issue quickly with team members (strong alternative to 'have a quick call' or 'talk online').
example: Let's jump into a quick huddle to resolve this blocker before the end of the day.

meaning: To distribute data or send a single message to all users on a network or channel simultaneously (strong alternative to 'send to everyone' or 'tell everyone').
example: The CEO will broadcast the announcement to the entire company channel.

meaning: To notify a specific user in a digital thread by using the '@' symbol followed by their username (strong alternative to 'tag someone' or 'call them out by name').
example: Be sure to mention the designer in your comment so they get an immediate notification.

meaning: A grouped series of messages or replies that keep a specific digital conversation organized under one main post (strong alternative to 'chain of emails' or 'message history').
example: Let's move this conversation to a separate thread to keep the main channel clean.

meaning: To work jointly with others in a shared digital workspace on a single project or document in real time (strong alternative to 'work together' or 'edit together').
example: Five team members can collaborate on this presentation simultaneously using Google Slides.

meaning: To officially send a message, notification, or package of data to its destination (strong alternative to 'send off' or 'mail').
example: The system will automatically dispatch a confirmation email once payment is processed.

meaning: To formally confirm receipt of a digital communication or notification (strong alternative to 'say I saw it' or 'got it').
example: Please acknowledge the receipt of the client's brief by replying to their email.

meaning: To transfer an unresolved customer issue or technical bug to a higher tier of authority or support (strong alternative to 'pass it up' or 'tell the boss').
example: If the first-level support cannot resolve the issue, they will escalate it to the engineering team.

meaning: To mark an email, message, or file with a visual indicator for priority or follow-up action (strong alternative to 'mark as important' or 'remember to check').
example: I will flag this email so I remember to draft a response first thing tomorrow.

meaning: To temporarily silence notifications or audio from a specific user, channel, or application (strong alternative to 'turn off sounds' or 'make silent').
example: I had to mute the group chat because the continuous alerts were distracting me during the meeting.

meaning: To assign tasks, action items, or access permissions to another team member using a project management tool (strong alternative to 'pass the work' or 'give the task').
example: You can delegate the database maintenance task to our junior administrator through Jira.

meaning: To explicitly choose or give consent to participate in an email list, data sharing, or feature trial (strong alternative to 'say yes to emails' or 'sign up').
example: Users must opt-in to receive promotional offers via email.

meaning: To explicitly choose to withdraw from or stop receiving a digital service, email list, or tracking program (strong alternative to 'unsubscribe' or 'stop getting emails').
example: You can opt-out of cookie tracking by adjusting your browser privacy settings.

meaning: To direct communication or focus user traffic toward a specific digital destination or medium (strong alternative to 'send traffic to' or 'point people to').
example: We should channel all customer inquiries through our online helpdesk rather than email.

meaning: To send signals, messages, or data packets over a communication medium or network (strong alternative to 'send data' or 'pass along information').
example: The satellite will transmit real-time telemetry data to the ground station.

meaning: To ask for or request information, feedback, or input from users via digital channels (strong alternative to 'ask for' or 'beg for feedback').
example: We will solicit customer feedback through a post-purchase survey.

meaning: To monitor and control user-generated content or discussions to ensure they follow guidelines (strong alternative to 'watch and filter posts' or 'police the chat').
example: Our community manager will moderate the forum to prevent spam and offensive comments.

meaning: To move completed or historical data to a separate, long-term storage location without deleting it (strong alternative to 'hide old files' or 'save in a safe place').
example: Instead of deleting old project folders, we archive them to keep the workspace organized.

meaning: To permanently and irreversibly remove unwanted data, files, or database records to free up space (strong alternative to 'delete completely' or 'wipe out').
example: The system is configured to purge deleted emails after thirty days.

meaning: To reduce the file size of data or media using an algorithm, making it easier to send or store (strong alternative to 'make file size smaller' or 'zip a file').
example: You should compress the high-resolution images before uploading them to the web server.

meaning: To retrieve and decompress specific files from a compressed archive, or to pull specific data from a database (strong alternative to 'unzip' or 'pull out data').
example: First extract the ZIP folder to your desktop, then run the installer.

meaning: To transfer data from an external file or format into a software application (strong alternative to 'bring data in' or 'load a file').
example: You can import your contact list directly from a CSV file into the CRM system.

meaning: To convert and transfer data from a software application into a file format compatible with other software (strong alternative to 'save file as something else' or 'send data out').
example: Once you finish editing the spreadsheet, export it as a PDF to share with the client.

meaning: To search for and successfully recall specific data or files from a storage system or database (strong alternative to 'find a file' or 'search and get').
example: The search tool makes it easy to retrieve documents using keywords.

meaning: To create an exact replica of a file, folder, or database record in the same directory or system (strong alternative to 'copy-paste' or 'make a copy').
example: Please duplicate the template folder before you begin customizing it for the new client.

meaning: To make a complete, identical copy of a software project, repository, or virtual environment (strong alternative to 'copy a whole setup' or 'make a twin').
example: Developers need to clone the repository to their local machines to begin coding.

meaning: To import and absorb large datasets into a database or data warehouse for processing and analysis (strong alternative to 'bring lots of data in' or 'load huge files').
example: The analytics platform is designed to ingest millions of data points per second.

meaning: To combine multiple sources of data, files, or information into a single, unified database or report (strong alternative to 'put spreadsheets together' or 'combine data').
example: We will consolidate all customer feedback spreadsheets into one central database.

meaning: To move data, applications, or entire operating environments from one platform, server, or system to another (strong alternative to 'move files over' or 'transfer databases').
example: The company plans to migrate its local database to the cloud next month.

meaning: To create a duplicate copy of files or system configurations to protect against data loss (strong alternative to 'save on external disk' or 'copy files just in case').
example: Remember to backup your work daily to the cloud storage.

meaning: To continuously copy and synchronize database changes across multiple servers to ensure high availability (strong alternative to 'copy databases automatically').
example: The system replicates data across three separate server locations to prevent downtime.

meaning: To analyze a string of code, file, or text by breaking it down into component parts to understand its structure (strong alternative to 'read through text' or 'look through code').
example: The script will parse the raw JSON data and display it in a user-friendly table.

meaning: A formal request for specific information from a database using structured commands (strong alternative to 'search database' or 'ask database for info').
example: You can run a SQL query to find out how many users signed up last month.

meaning: To organize and catalog database records or web pages to optimize search speed and retrieval efficiency (strong alternative to 'make database search fast' or 'make a search list').
example: Search engines index new web pages so they can be retrieved quickly by searchers.

meaning: To divide a storage drive or large database into distinct, independent sections for better management (strong alternative to 'split a drive' or 'cut database in half').
example: We will partition the hard drive to separate the operating system from personal files.

meaning: To write new data over existing data, completely replacing the old file or database record (strong alternative to 'save on top of' or 'replace old file').
example: Be careful not to overwrite the original document with your changes.

meaning: To shorten a data string or file by cutting off its end after a specific limit (strong alternative to 'cut off' or 'shorten').
example: The system will truncate usernames that exceed fifteen characters.

meaning: To temporarily hold data in computer memory while transferring it from one place to another, preventing interruptions (strong alternative to 'load ahead of time' or 'hold data').
example: The video player will buffer a few seconds of video to ensure smooth playback.

meaning: To narrow down a list of data or search results by applying specific conditions or criteria (strong alternative to 'narrow down search' or 'sort out').
example: You can filter the customer list to show only those who bought a product in the last month.

meaning: To arrange data, files, or records in a specific alphabetical, numerical, or chronological order (strong alternative to 'put in order' or 'line up').
example: You can sort the files by date modified to find the most recent ones.

meaning: To link database fields or values from one system to corresponding fields in another system (strong alternative to 'match up lists' or 'connect fields').
example: You need to map the customer name field in the CSV file to the CRM's name field.

meaning: To check if user input or data meets specific rules or formats before accepting it (strong alternative to 'check if input is correct' or 'confirm entry').
example: The form will validate the email address to ensure it contains an '@' symbol.

meaning: To unpack a compressed file or archive back into its original size and format (strong alternative to 'unzip' or 'make file normal size').
example: The utility will decompress the backup files automatically during restoration.

meaning: To set the starting values or prepare a system, program, or hardware device for operation (strong alternative to 'turn on' or 'set up from scratch').
example: You must initialize the new hard drive before you can write data to it.

meaning: To start up a computer's operating system, loading all necessary files into memory (strong alternative to 'turn on the computer').
example: It takes only ten seconds for the laptop to boot from the solid-state drive.

meaning: To forcefully stop or end a running system process, application, or network connection (strong alternative to 'shut down program' or 'kill a process').
example: You may need to terminate the frozen application using the Task Manager.

meaning: To arrange or set up the hardware, software, or network settings to make a system work in a specific way (strong alternative to 'change settings' or 'set up').
example: Our team will configure the router to enable secure remote access.

meaning: To adjust a device, monitor, or sensor to conform to a standard or ensure precise measurements (strong alternative to 'adjust screen' or 'make device accurate').
example: The technician will calibrate the monitor to display accurate colors for video editing.

meaning: To prepare and equip a server, user account, or network resource with all necessary tools and access rights (strong alternative to 'set up account' or 'give a new setup').
example: IT will provision a new laptop with the company software for the incoming employee.

meaning: To set up a digital system or script to perform repetitive tasks automatically without human intervention (strong alternative to 'make it run by itself').
example: We can automate the daily reports so they are sent to the team every evening.

meaning: To declare a software feature, code, or hardware model outdated and scheduled for replacement, though still available (strong alternative to 'make outdated' or 'stop supporting').
example: We plan to deprecate the older version of our API by the end of the year.

meaning: To move a software application, updates, or a website from development to a live production server (strong alternative to 'put online' or 'launch website').
example: We plan to deploy the new checkout feature to the live website tonight.

meaning: To refine code, databases, or system settings to make them run with maximum speed and minimum resource usage (strong alternative to 'make computer run faster' or 'speed up').
example: The developers will optimize the page code to improve loading speeds on mobile devices.

meaning: To restart a computer, device, or system to apply updates or clear software glitches (strong alternative to 'turn off and on again').
example: You must reboot your computer to complete the installation of the software update.

meaning: To automatically record a chronological history of events, errors, or activities occurring in an application or system (strong alternative to 'write down what happened' or 'keep records of server').
example: The server will log every login attempt, allowing us to track unauthorized access.

meaning: To adjust the capacity of a computing system or server cluster to handle more work or traffic (strong alternative to 'make setup bigger' or 'make servers stronger').
example: Cloud hosting makes it easy to scale our resources during peak shopping seasons.

meaning: To translate a domain name (like website.com) into an IP address that computers can understand, or to fix a network path (strong alternative to 'translate website address' or 'connect domain').
example: The DNS server could not resolve the website's address, resulting in an error page.

meaning: To identify the root cause of a technical problem, error, or system slowdown (strong alternative to 'find out what's wrong' or 'check for problems').
example: Run the built-in troubleshooter to diagnose any issues with your network connection.

meaning: To run standardized tests on hardware or software to evaluate and compare its performance (strong alternative to 'test speed' or 'test computer performance').
example: We benchmarked the new processors to see how they compare to the older model.

meaning: To modify a software interface, theme, or feature to suit the individual user's preferences (strong alternative to 'change how it looks' or 'make it personal').
example: You can customize your dashboard by dragging and dropping widgets.

meaning: To continuously watch and check the health, performance, and security of a system or network in real-time (strong alternative to 'watch the server' or 'keep an eye on systems').
example: We use automated dashboards to monitor server CPU usage and alert us of anomalies.

meaning: To restore a device, program, or system settings to its original default factory state (strong alternative to 'wipe out and start over' or 'clear settings').
example: If the router stops working, you might need to reset it to its default settings.

meaning: To run automated tests or checks to prove that a system function, backup, or file is correct and working (strong alternative to 'double-check' or 'make sure it works').
example: Let's run a test query to verify that the database update was successful.

meaning: To provide server space, resources, and services for a website or application to be accessible on the internet (strong alternative to 'put online' or 'keep on a computer').
example: We will host our new e-commerce application on AWS cloud servers.

meaning: To create an exact, real-time copy of a website, screen, or hard drive on another server or display (strong alternative to 'copy everything exactly' or 'duplicate screen').
example: The repository has a mirror site in Europe to ensure fast downloads for local developers.

meaning: To proactively replace a default setting or automatic value with a custom manual entry or command (strong alternative to 'force-change' or 'cancel default setting').
example: You can override the default template by uploading your own custom CSS file.

meaning: To systematically analyze a problem or bug to find its source and correct it (strong alternative to 'try to fix' or 'look for the problem').
example: Please troubleshoot the network issue before calling the IT support line.

meaning: To replace a software version or hardware component with a newer, faster, or more powerful version (strong alternative to 'get a newer version' or 'buy better hardware').
example: We need to upgrade our server RAM to support the growing number of active users.

meaning: To reduce the capacity, size, or dimensions of a system, server setup, or image file (strong alternative to 'make server smaller' or 'reduce setup size').
example: The system will automatically downscale the server count when traffic returns to normal.

meaning: To formally take a server, software application, or hardware device out of active service (strong alternative to 'shut down old server permanently' or 'take offline forever').
example: The company will decommission the legacy email server at the end of the year.

meaning: To write and save a set of code changes to a version control repository (like Git) with a descriptive message (strong alternative to 'save your changes' or 'record code changes').
example: Remember to commit your code changes before pushing them to the remote server.

meaning: The process of locating, analyzing, and fixing bugs or defects in software code (strong alternative to 'find and fix errors' or 'clean code errors').
example: The developer spent the afternoon debugging the login form to resolve the error.

meaning: To connect different software programs or systems so they can share data and work together seamlessly (strong alternative to 'connect systems' or 'hook up programs').
example: The goal is to integrate the CRM with our email marketing tool.

meaning: To repeat a design, development, or testing process, making improvements with each cycle (strong alternative to 'try again' or 'repeat process with changes').
example: We will iterate on the app's user interface based on the first round of user feedback.

meaning: To restructure existing computer code to improve its readability, structure, and efficiency without changing its behavior (strong alternative to 'clean up code' or 'rewrite code').
example: It is good practice to refactor your code to make it easier to maintain in the future.

meaning: To automatically extract large amounts of public data or text from websites using a computer program (strong alternative to 'copy text off website manually' or 'grab web text').
example: The tool will scrape product prices from competitor sites to compare them automatically.

meaning: To combine different branches of code or document versions into a single, unified file (strong alternative to 'combine files' or 'put code branches together').
example: The team lead will merge the feature branch into the main development line.

meaning: To convert written programming code into a machine-readable format that a computer can run directly (strong alternative to 'make code run' or 'turn code into software').
example: The compiler will translate your C++ code into an executable file.

meaning: To release a small software update designed to fix a bug or security vulnerability quickly (strong alternative to 'put a quick fix on' or 'hotfix').
example: The developer released a hotfix patch to resolve the crash issue reported by users.

meaning: To run a model of a real-world system or user behavior on a computer to test outcomes (strong alternative to 'test by pretending' or 'fake a run').
example: We can simulate high user traffic to see how the website handles heavy loads.

meaning: To restore a database or system to a previous stable state, undoing recent changes that caused errors (strong alternative to 'undo update' or 'go back to old version').
example: Due to a bug in the new release, we had to rollback the system to yesterday's version.

meaning: The process of generating an image or visual interface on a screen from a digital model or code (strong alternative to 'draw image' or 'show on screen').
example: It takes a few seconds for the graphics card to render the 3D model on screen.

meaning: To view how a webpage, email, or document will look to end-users before publishing it (strong alternative to 'look at before live' or 'test look').
example: Always preview your newsletter draft to check the layout before hitting send.

meaning: To organize or style digital text, files, or storage media according to a specific structure or layout (strong alternative to 'clean up layout' or 'arrange text design').
example: You should format the document with consistent headings and fonts for a professional look.

meaning: To create a simulated version of a software component, database, or API for testing purposes (strong alternative to 'pretend link' or 'fake API connection').
example: We will mock the payment gateway response so we can test checkout without spending real money.

meaning: To create an independent copy of an existing software repository to develop it separately (strong alternative to 'split project copy' or 'branch off project').
example: You can fork the open-source project to add your custom features.

meaning: To automatically analyze code for potential errors, stylistic bugs, or bad practices before running it (strong alternative to 'check code for mistakes' or 'clean code typos').
example: Always run a linter to lint your Javascript code before submitting a pull request.

meaning: The process of compiling, linking, and packaging raw source code into a completed, runnable software release (strong alternative to 'make final software' or 'generate executable').
example: The automated build pipeline failed due to a missing dependency.

meaning: To convert plain data into a coded, unreadable format that can only be unlocked with a key (strong alternative to 'scramble data' or 'lock with password').
example: We encrypt all customer passwords in the database to prevent identity theft.

meaning: To translate encrypted data back into its original, readable format using a key (strong alternative to 'unscramble data' or 'unlock files').
example: The software will decrypt the files automatically once you enter the security key.

meaning: To verify the identity of a user, device, or system before granting access to a network or app (strong alternative to 'check login details' or 'prove who you are').
example: The system will authenticate your identity using a password and a fingerprint scan.

meaning: To give official permission or access rights to a user or system to perform a specific action (strong alternative to 'give permission' or 'let someone in').
example: The system administrator will authorize you to view confidential files.

meaning: A list of email addresses, IP addresses, or websites that are blocked due to security risks or spam (strong alternative to 'ban list' or 'block list').
example: The firewall will blacklist any IP address that attempts to log in unsuccessfully multiple times.

meaning: A list of approved email addresses, IP addresses, or applications that are always allowed access (strong alternative to 'allow list' or 'safe list').
example: We whitelist the IP addresses of our employees to allow them to access the network from home.

meaning: To conduct a formal, systematic review of digital logs, access history, and security settings to ensure safety compliance (strong alternative to 'check security records' or 'look at security logs').
example: The security team will audit our system logs to check for any unauthorized access.

meaning: To avoid or find an alternative route around a network restriction, security gate, or system check (strong alternative to 'go around block' or 'skip restriction').
example: Users are not allowed to bypass the firewall using unauthorized VPNs.

meaning: To officially cancel or take back access rights, permissions, or digital certificates from a user (strong alternative to 'take away access' or 'cancel permission').
example: IT will revoke network access immediately once an employee leaves the company.

meaning: To hide or obscure sensitive data (like credit card numbers or passwords) on screen with characters like asterisks (strong alternative to 'hide numbers' or 'hide password text').
example: The application will mask your password on the screen as you type it.

meaning: To clean user input data by removing potentially harmful code or characters before it reaches the database (strong alternative to 'clean input' or 'scrub text fields').
example: You must sanitize all input fields to protect the website from SQL injection attacks.

meaning: To disconnect an infected device or file from the rest of the network to prevent the spread of malware (strong alternative to 'separate computer' or 'disconnect virus').
example: The antivirus software will isolate the suspicious file in a secure quarantine folder.

meaning: To limit access to certain files, directories, or network areas to specific users (strong alternative to 'block off' or 'limit access').
example: We restrict access to the payroll database to the HR department only.

meaning: To isolate a file identified as containing malware to prevent it from affecting the operating system (strong alternative to 'put file in safe jail folder' or 'block virus file').
example: The security program will quarantine the virus before it can infect other files.

meaning: To systematically clean a dataset of personal identifier details or clean databases of corruption errors (strong alternative to 'wipe personal details' or 'clean dataset').
example: We need to scrub the customer data to comply with privacy laws before sharing it with analysts.

meaning: To intentionally slow down the speed of a network connection or processor to manage resource limits (strong alternative to 'slow down connection' or 'limit bandwidth').
example: The server will throttle user requests if they exceed the rate limit of 100 requests per minute.

meaning: To move through a website, application, or directory structure using menus, buttons, and links (strong alternative to 'click around' or 'go through a site').
example: The website layout is designed to help visitors navigate from page to page easily.

meaning: To actively engage with elements on a digital screen, such as buttons, sliders, or input fields (strong alternative to 'click or type on page').
example: Users can interact with the dynamic charts to filter data by date.

meaning: To reduce an open window or application to its smallest form, keeping it running in the taskbar (strong alternative to 'make window small' or 'hide window').
example: You can minimize the browser window to see your desktop background.

meaning: To expand an application window to fill the entire computer screen (strong alternative to 'make window big' or 'fill screen').
example: Maximize the video player to watch the training tutorial in full-screen mode.

meaning: To move text or graphics vertically or horizontally across a screen to view unseen parts of a page (strong alternative to 'move down the page' or 'go down').
example: Users must scroll to the bottom of the page to read the privacy policy.

meaning: To tailor a digital service, layout, or content feed to fit an individual user's preferences or profile (strong alternative to 'change settings to make it mine' or 'make custom layout').
example: The app uses algorithms to personalize recommendations on your home screen.

meaning: To switch between two options or states (like light mode and dark mode) with a single click (strong alternative to 'switch on or off' or 'click the button to switch').
example: You can toggle the dark mode option in the settings menu.

meaning: To position the mouse cursor over an on-screen element without clicking it to reveal more options (strong alternative to 'put cursor on' or 'hold mouse over').
example: Hover your mouse over the icon to see a short tooltip explaining its function.

meaning: To slide a finger across a touch screen to scroll through items or navigate between pages (strong alternative to 'slide your finger' or 'slide the screen').
example: You can swipe left to view the next slide in the photo gallery.

meaning: To magnify or reduce the size of an image, text, or page display on a screen (strong alternative to 'make bigger or smaller on screen' or 'blow up display').
example: You can zoom in to read the small print in the PDF document.

meaning: To click a button or menu to reveal hidden options or content on a webpage (strong alternative to 'open up menu' or 'show folder contents').
example: Click the arrow to expand the menu and see more subfolders.

meaning: To hide or fold away options or text sections to clean up the screen space (strong alternative to 'hide menu section' or 'fold it up').
example: You can collapse the sidebar to give yourself more screen space to write.

meaning: To automatically send a visitor from one URL address to a different webpage (strong alternative to 'send to another page' or 'send automatically to a link').
example: The website will redirect you to the login page if your session expires.

meaning: To insert a video, image, map, or code component directly into a webpage's code so it displays in place (strong alternative to 'put video inside page' or 'add map to site').
example: You can embed the YouTube video directly into your blog post using an iframe.

meaning: To reload the current webpage or application display to load the latest data or content (strong alternative to 'reload the page' or 're-run page').
example: If the page loads incorrectly, click the refresh button in your browser.

meaning: To make digital content, a blog post, or a website update publicly accessible to users (strong alternative to 'make it so people can see' or 'put it out online').
example: We will publish the new pricing page once it is approved by the director.

meaning: To monitor and record user actions, visits, or metrics on a website or app for analysis (strong alternative to 'watch what they do' or 'follow online actions').
example: We use cookies to track user engagement and analyze website traffic.

meaning: An action performed by a software program to retrieve data from a remote server or database (strong alternative to 'go get data' or 'grab data').
example: The application will fetch the current weather data from the API.

meaning: To create a hypermedia connection between two digital items, such as webpages, files, or database records (strong alternative to 'connect files' or 'make a connection').
example: You should link the terms of service page in the footer of the website.

meaning: To attach a keyword or metadata label to a digital piece of content, file, or photo to make it easily searchable (strong alternative to 'put a label on' or 'mark file keyword').
example: Be sure to tag each blog post with relevant topics like SEO and marketing.

meaning: To arrange emails, data packets, or server tasks in a sequential line to be processed one after the other (strong alternative to 'put in line to wait' or 'make data wait in line').
example: The server will queue the outgoing emails and process them ten at a time.

meaning: To record or save digital data, screens, or user input in real-time (strong alternative to 'grab screen' or 'take photo of screen').
example: Use the snippet tool to capture a screenshot of the error message.

meaning: To trim or cut off the outer edges of a digital image to improve framing or reduce size (strong alternative to 'cut image edges' or 'trim borders').
example: Please crop the team photo to focus on the speakers.

meaning: To transfer data or files from a local computer or device to a remote server or website (strong alternative to 'send file to site' or 'put file on portal').
example: You can upload your profile picture through the settings page.

meaning: To transfer files or data from a remote server or internet site to a local device (strong alternative to 'get file from site' or 'save file from internet').
example: Users can download the user manual in PDF format directly from our website.

meaning: Short for synchronize, to keep data consistent across different folders, drives, or apps (strong alternative to 'make match' or 'keep same').
example: Make sure you sync your local drafts to the cloud server before shutting down.

meaning: To store files, images, or web assets on a server so they can be viewed on the web (strong alternative to 'put images online' or 'keep files on server').
example: We can host our marketing graphics on a content delivery network for faster load times.

meaning: To transmit audio or video content continuously in real-time over the internet without downloading the full file first (strong alternative to 'play video online' or 'watch online without saving').
example: The conference will stream the keynote speech live on our portal.
